# Lintel Catalog

> A curated catalog of JSON Schemas for validating configuration files, hosted at https://catalog.lintel.tools/

Lintel Catalog maps over 1,000 JSON Schemas to the filenames they validate. Given a filename like `tsconfig.json` or `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, the catalog tells you which schema to use for validation and editor autocompletion.

## How to find the schema for a file

### Option 1: Search catalog.json directly

Fetch the full catalog index:

    GET https://catalog.lintel.tools/catalog.json

This returns a JSON object with a `schemas` array. Each entry has:

- `name` — human-readable schema name
- `description` — what the schema validates
- `url` — the schema URL (fetch this to get the full JSON Schema)
- `fileMatch` — glob patterns for filenames this schema applies to
- `versions` — (optional) map of version identifiers to versioned schema URLs

To find the schema for a file, match its path against the `fileMatch` globs. Patterns without a `/` match at any directory depth (i.e., `tsconfig.json` matches `project/tsconfig.json`). Patterns with `**` match any number of path segments.

Example: to find the schema for `biome.json`, look for an entry whose `fileMatch` contains `biome.json`. The result's `url` field gives you the schema to fetch.

### Option 2: Fetch a schema directly by URL convention

Schemas are hosted at predictable URLs:

    https://catalog.lintel.tools/schemas/{group}/{schema}/latest.json

For a specific version:

    https://catalog.lintel.tools/schemas/{group}/{schema}/versions/{version}.json

Example:

    https://catalog.lintel.tools/schemas/biome/biome/latest.json
    https://catalog.lintel.tools/schemas/biome/biome/versions/2.1.0.json

### Option 3: Browse the site

Visit https://catalog.lintel.tools/ to browse schemas by group, search by name, and view rendered documentation for each schema.

## Catalog format

The catalog follows the SchemaStore catalog format (compatible with VS Code, IntelliJ, and other editors). The structure:

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://catalog.lintel.tools/schemas/lintel/catalog/latest.json",
  "version": 1,
  "title": "Lintel Catalog",
  "schemas": [
    {
      "name": "Configuration",
      "description": "Biome configuration file",
      "url": "https://catalog.lintel.tools/schemas/biome/biome/latest.json",
      "sourceUrl": "https://biomejs.dev/schemas/latest/schema.json",
      "fileMatch": ["biome.json", "biome.jsonc"],
      "versions": {
        "2.1.0": "https://catalog.lintel.tools/schemas/biome/biome/versions/2.1.0.json"
      }
    }
  ],
  "groups": [
    {
      "name": "Biome",
      "description": "Biome configuration schemas",
      "schemas": ["Configuration"]
    }
  ]
}
```

## Lintel CLI

Lintel is a multi-format JSON Schema linter. Install and run it via npx:

    npx @lintel/lintel <command>

### explain — Schema documentation

Show rendered documentation for a schema, navigable by JSON Pointer or JSONPath:

    npx @lintel/lintel explain tsconfig.json
    npx @lintel/lintel explain --schema https://catalog.lintel.tools/schemas/biome/biome/latest.json
    npx @lintel/lintel explain tsconfig.json /properties/compilerOptions
    npx @lintel/lintel explain biome.json '$.linter'

When given a data file, `explain` also validates it and shows errors inline in the documentation. Use `--path` to resolve a schema by filename without requiring the file to exist:

    npx @lintel/lintel explain --path .github/workflows/ci.yml

### identify — Schema resolution

Show which schema a file resolves to without validating:

    npx @lintel/lintel identify package.json

### check — Validate files

Validate files against their matched schemas with pretty terminal output:

    npx @lintel/lintel check *.json
    npx @lintel/lintel check biome.json tsconfig.json

### Other commands

- `ci` — Validate with machine-readable one-error-per-line output
- `convert` — Convert between JSON, YAML, and TOML formats
- `format` — Format JSON, YAML, TOML, and Markdown files
- `annotate` — Add schema URL annotations (modelines) to files
- `init` — Create a lintel.toml configuration file

## Key URLs

- Catalog index: https://catalog.lintel.tools/catalog.json
- Site: https://catalog.lintel.tools/
- Source: https://github.com/lintel-rs/catalog
- Lintel linter: https://github.com/lintel-rs/lintel
